channel.title: DigestFlow Sample Feed
channel.description: Sample RSS feed for local DigestFlow testing.

item.title: AI briefing workflow cut research prep, but editors still blocked publish risk
item.description: An internal communications team reduced manual research preparation from 6 hours to 2.5 hours after introducing an AI briefing workflow for weekly updates. The gain held only when editors reviewed every draft against source links, because unsupported claims still slipped into early versions when the team rushed publication.

item.title: Structured intake forms improved support triage, but bad labels still broke handoffs
item.description: A support operations group cut escalation triage time by 28 percent after replacing free-form intake messages with structured forms and AI-assisted summaries. Even so, routing mistakes continued when ticket labels were inconsistent, which forced human coordinators to correct handoffs before specialist teams could act.

item.title: Reusable content components reduced rewrites only after validation was added
item.description: A content operations team reused post templates, source citation blocks, and CTA modules across multiple internal publishing flows. Rewrite cycles dropped from 4 rounds to 2 only after the team added validation checks for source accuracy; before that, reusable components sped drafting up but also amplified the same factual mistakes across outputs.

item.title: Automation stalled when teams skipped process mapping and automated unclear reviews
item.description: Several operations teams tried to automate approval chains before documenting who owned each review step. The result was a faster system on paper but slower real execution, because unresolved handoffs created duplicate checks, unclear escalation paths, and manual workarounds whenever the AI output reached a disputed reviewer.

item.title: Teams saw better AI results after redesigning the workflow, not just adding a model
item.description: A cross-functional operations program compared two rollout approaches across 3 business units. Teams that redesigned workflow steps before introducing AI reduced review cycle time by 35 percent and improved source traceability, while teams that added the model on top of existing messy processes reported faster drafting but little improvement in final decision speed.
